status.title: Status
status.tooltipClipboardDisabledWs: Clipboard monitoring is disabled because text is being received via WebSocket.
status.tooltipObsConnected: OBS is connected. Click to open.
status.tooltipObsDisconnected: OBS is disconnected. Click to open.

obs.title: Active Game Capture
obs.runCaptureWizardTooltip: Open text capture setup for the selected game.
obs.setupCaptureTooltip:
Setup Capture adds the selected Window Capture or Game Capture source for windows, or Video Capture Device for capture cards and cameras.
Use Game Capture for fullscreen games; use Window Capture when Game Capture shows black.
On Linux/Steam Deck, Setup Capture currently uses XComposite Window Capture.
Capture-card game captures are created without automatic window-title switching.
obs.captureCardTooltip: When enabled, probes for connected capture cards and cameras in addition to application windows.
obs.notSupportedOS: Need to set up game capture in OBS on this OS.

rename.title: Rename Game Capture
rename.description: Only the game capture name will change. Sources will stay as they are.

overlay.title: Overlay
overlay.runOnStartupTooltip: Start the GSM overlay automatically when GSM starts.

actions.title: Utilities
actions.texthookerTooltip: Open the Text Feed for game text capture
actions.overlayTooltip: Launch the Yomitan overlay for in-game lookups
actions.overlayPlatformWarning: Overlay support on this platform is experimental.

ankiBeacon.tooltipConnected: AnkiBeacon is running. GSM will listen for new cards instead of polling in the background.
ankiBeacon.tooltipMissing: Anki is connected. Click to install AnkiBeacon for lighter new-card detection.
ankiBeacon.title: Install AnkiBeacon
ankiBeacon.description: AnkiBeacon is a small optional Anki add-on that tells GSM when a new card is created.
ankiBeacon.benefit: With it installed, GSM can grab new cards from Anki directly, using up to 98% less background checking while you play.
ankiBeacon.installStarted: AnkiBeacon was opened. Confirm the add-on install in Anki, then restart Anki if it asks.
ankiBeacon.installFailed: Automatic install did not open in Anki.
ankiBeacon.manualInstall: Copy the AnkiWeb code and paste it in Anki → Tools → Add-ons → Get Add-ons.

stats.loading: Waiting for GSM stats to load...

console.exportLogsTooltip: Export logs to a zip archive
console.modeSimpleTooltip: Switch to Advanced logs (shows basic + background logs)
console.modeAdvancedTooltip: Switch to Simple logs (hides background logs)

cuda.title: CUDA Installation
cuda.installDetail:
This installs onnxruntime-gpu for faster processing.

- Requires additional storage
- May not be compatible with all systems
- Install at your own risk
cuda.uninstallDetail: This uninstalls onnxruntime-gpu and can fix GPU-related issues.

management.title: GSM Management
management.resetDetail: This runs "uv sync" and restores dependency versions from the lockfile.

welcome.title: Welcome to GameSentenceMiner! 🎮
welcome.description: GSM helps you automatically create Anki flashcards from games you play. It captures text, audio, and screenshots to build high-quality cards for Language learning.
welcome.hint: This wizard will walk you through the basics. You can always change settings later.

textSources.title: Text Input Sources 📝
textSources.description: GSM needs a way to read text from your game. Here are the most common options:
textSources.agentDesc: Works with most Visual Novels. Connects automatically via websocket on port 9001.
textSources.ocrDesc: Use screen OCR for games where text hooking doesn't work. Configure in the OCR tab after setup.
textSources.clipboardDesc: If your text source copies to clipboard, GSM can read from there automatically.
textSources.hint: Websocket sources (Agent, Textractor, LunaTranslator) can be managed in Settings → General.

connectivity.title: Connectivity Check 🔗
connectivity.description: Let's verify your external tools are reachable:
connectivity.ankiFailedPre: Not reachable. Make sure Anki is open with the
connectivity.obsConnected: Connected! OBS WebSocket server is running.
connectivity.obsFailed: Not reachable. OBS is optional — you can set it up later in Settings.
connectivity.hint: You can continue even if connections fail — configure them later in Settings.

finish.title: You're All Set! 🎉
finish.description: GSM is ready to go. Here are some tips to get started:
finish.tipHome: Open the Home tab to see the current game text stream
finish.tipGameSettings: Use Game Automation to configure per-game automation
finish.tipSettings: Check the Settings tab for Anki, audio, and hotkey configuration
finish.tipConsole: The Console tab shows what GSM is doing behind the scenes
finish.guidePre: For detailed guides and troubleshooting, visit the

captureWizard.title: Text Capture Wizard (WIP)
captureWizard.subtitle: Finish text input, OCR fallback, and automation for this game.

preview.title: Check the OBS preview
preview.description: GSM refreshes this preview every second and fits the source to the OBS canvas before each screenshot.
preview.noPreview: No OBS preview is available yet.

agent.title: Try an Agent script first
agent.description: Agent scripts usually give cleaner text than OCR when a matching game script exists.
agent.nsHint: Scripts starting with NS_ are for Nintendo Switch emulator targets.
agent.switchDetected: This looks like a Switch emulator target. NS_ script matches are expected.
agent.noMatches: No likely Agent scripts were found.
agent.searchPlaceholder: Search scripts by game or file name

hook.title: Try Luna or Textractor
hook.description: Luna and Textractor hooks are mainly for visual novels. For video games, an Agent script or OCR is more likely to work; only use this if you see clean dialogue.
hook.waiting: Waiting for hooks with text. Advance the game dialogue.
hook.startFirst: Start an engine to scan for hooks.

ocr.title: Set up OCR fallback
ocr.description: OCR is the most reliable option when no script or hook gives clean game text.
ocr.reasonStable: An OCR area keeps scans focused on the dialogue box.
ocr.reasonNoise: Smaller areas avoid menus, names, and UI noise.
ocr.reasonFallback: It still works for console games, protected engines, and capture cards.

profile.title: Save this game setup
profile.description: Save automation for this OBS scene and optionally open GSM Profiles for a larger per-game profile split.
profile.saveAutomation: Save Game Automation for this scene

finish.title: Setup saved
finish.description: You can test the source now or review the saved automation for this scene.

hub.noResults: No matching settings found. Try broader words like anki, hotkey, overlay, OBS, or audio.

tooltips.textractor32: Path to 32-bit Textractor executable. Used for 32-bit game targets when available.
tooltips.lunaPath: Path to LunaTranslator executable. Used when game launcher is set to LunaTranslator.

tooltips.ocrAuto: Start continuous Auto OCR when this game is active.
tooltips.ocrManual: Start manual OCR runtime for this game. OCR capture remains manual via controls/hotkeys.

sceneAndAreas.title: Game & Areas
sceneAndAreas.noScenes: Set Up Capture in Home Tab
sceneAndAreas.noScenesHint: Setup Capture in the Home tab before configuring OCR areas.

settings.title: OCR Settings
settings.textAppearsInstantlyWarning: Warning: Only turn this on when the full text appears instantly. If text reveals gradually or animates in, this can get very messy.

duplicateThreshold.label: Duplicate Match Threshold (%)
duplicateThreshold.title: Higher values are stricter. Raise this if different lines are being treated as duplicates.

changeThreshold.label: Text Change Threshold (%)
changeThreshold.title: Lower values trigger OCR2 sooner on partial changes. Higher values wait for clearer changes.

evolvingPrefix.label: Evolving Prefix Match (%)
evolvingPrefix.title: Used when checking whether a short line looks like the beginning of a longer line.

truncationFallbackMin.label: Truncation Fallback Min Threshold (%)
truncationFallbackMin.title: Minimum compare threshold required before prefix or suffix truncation matching is allowed.

truncationStrictMin.label: Truncation Strict Threshold (%)
truncationStrictMin.title: Above this threshold, GSM also requires a stronger base similarity before treating a truncation as the same text.

truncationMargin.label: Truncation Base Margin
truncationMargin.title: How far below the compare threshold the base similarity may be before truncation fallback is rejected.

truncationMinLength.label: Truncation Min Length (chars)
truncationMinLength.title: Shortest normalized string length allowed for truncation matching.

truncationMinRatio.label: Truncation Min Length Ratio (%)
truncationMinRatio.title: How much of the longer line the shorter line must cover before truncation matching is considered.

subsetChunkMinLength.label: Subset Chunk Min Length (chars)
subsetChunkMinLength.title: Incoming OCR chunks shorter than this skip the chunk-coverage fallback unless they are exact matches.

shortChunkLimit.label: Short Chunk Limit (chars)
shortChunkLimit.title: Chunks at or below this length use the smaller matching-block minimum.

shortChunkMinSize.label: Short Chunk Match Min Size
shortChunkMinSize.title: Minimum contiguous match size counted for short chunks.

defaultMinSize.label: Default Match Min Size
defaultMinSize.title: Minimum contiguous match size counted for longer chunks.

coverageFloor.label: Subset Coverage Floor (%)
coverageFloor.title: Lowest allowed chunk-coverage requirement for dedupe fallback.

coverageCeiling.label: Subset Coverage Ceiling (%)
coverageCeiling.title: Highest allowed chunk-coverage requirement for dedupe fallback.

coverageOffset.label: Coverage Threshold Offset
coverageOffset.title: Offset applied before converting the current compare threshold into a coverage requirement.

longestBlockMin.label: Longest Block Minimum (chars)
longestBlockMin.title: Minimum contiguous block size required in subset coverage mode.

longestBlockDivisor.label: Longest Block Divisor
longestBlockDivisor.title: Larger values reduce the required contiguous block length for longer chunks.

footer.needAreasFirst: Draw OCR areas for this game before starting {mode} OCR.

tooltips.scene: Select the OBS Scene to capture for OCR. You can make a new Scene in the Home tab. Each scene can have its own OCR rectangles and furigana sensitivity.
tooltips.refreshScenes: Reload the OBS scene list and re-read the active scene's OCR area file.
tooltips.selectAreas: Open the OCR area selector for the current scene. Draw the rectangles GSM should scan.
tooltips.importAreas: Import OCR area config from your clipboard (JSON). Only the rectangle layout is imported.
tooltips.exportAreas: Copy the current scene's OCR rectangles to your clipboard as JSON.
tooltips.docs: Open the OCR guide documentation for setup help, engine notes, and workflows.
tooltips.advancedMode: Show engine selection and comparison tuning. Basic mode keeps common settings visible and hides the expert knobs.
tooltips.basicScanRate:
How often OCR scans in basic mode.
• As fast as possible (0): no scan delay; can expose UTF-8/encoding issues and uses the most CPU
• Fast: quick polling for responsive text
• Normal: balanced for most dialogue
• Slow/Very Slow: better for gradually revealed text
tooltips.advancedScanRate: OCR polling interval in seconds. Lower values scan more often and react faster, but use more resources. Higher values scan less often and may feel calmer on slower text.
tooltips.language: Select the language for OCR processing. This ensures only blocks with the correct characters are captured. All Latin-script languages (English, Spanish, French, German, etc.) are supported under English.
tooltips.baseScale:
Controls how much the screenshot is scaled before scanning.
• Lower = faster scans, less CPU, but may miss small text
• Higher = more accurate on small fonts, but uses more CPU

50% -> Fast | 75% -> Balanced | 100% -> Slowest

Tip: If OCR misses small kanji or skips characters, try increasing this value.
tooltips.furiganaFilter:
Filters characters smaller than the selected text size. If you notice real dialogue disappearing, either lower this or set it to 0.

Tip: Click Preview to open a window that helps you select the best sensitivity for your current game.
tooltips.furiganaPreview: Open a preview window to tune furigana sensitivity against a sample character from your current game.
tooltips.sendToClipboard: OCR sends text to GSM automatically. Choose which OCR modes should also copy the final text to the clipboard.
tooltips.sendToClipboardAuto: Copy normal automatic OCR results to the clipboard.
tooltips.sendToClipboardMenu: Copy menu OCR results to the clipboard.
tooltips.sendToClipboardAreaSelect: Copy manual area-select OCR captures to the clipboard.
tooltips.sendToWebsocket: Deprecated legacy output. OCR now sends results to GSM through IPC, and this direct OCR WebSocket output will be removed in a future patch. External output consumers should use ws://localhost:7275. The old OCR WebSocket port is configured in GSM Settings > Advanced.
tooltips.keepNewline: If enabled, OCR will attempt to keep line breaks in the output text for better readability. Not guaranteed.
tooltips.keepNewlineAuto: Keep line breaks for normal automatic OCR results.
tooltips.keepNewlineMenu: Keep line breaks for menu OCR results.
tooltips.keepNewlineAreaSelect: Keep line breaks for manual area-select OCR captures.
tooltips.textAppearsInstantly: Immediately run the main OCR when the stability OCR first sees new text or a new text box. Only use this for games where full dialogue appears instantly. Leave it off for typewriter-style, animated, or gradually appearing text, otherwise OCR output can get very messy.
tooltips.twoPassOCR: OCR Option 1 runs at the set scan rate. If two pass is enabled and the text does not change by the next scan, it will then do the second scan with the main OCR engine.
tooltips.stabilityOcr: This runs first to watch for text changes and stability. Fast engines work best here. On Windows, OneOCR is recommended.
tooltips.mainOcr: This engine sends the final OCR result to the websocket or clipboard. Pick the most accurate option that works well for your game.
tooltips.optimizeSecondScan: Trim the image for the second scan to improve performance (OneOCR 1st only). If your game's text is unusual and some text doesn't get captured, try turning this off.
tooltips.ocrScreenshots: If enabled, OCR will also process screenshots taken from the clipboard.
tooltips.manualHotkey: Hotkey to manually OCR the selected area. Uses the Main OCR engine, and is also used for menu OCR (Ctrl+Click in Area Selector). Press Escape to clear.
tooltips.areaSelectHotkey: On press, lets you select a temporary area to OCR one time. Useful for menus. Press Escape to clear.
tooltips.wholeWindowHotkey: Runs a one-time OCR scan on the full active OCR source (typically your OBS game source), bypassing area rectangles. Press Escape to clear.
tooltips.pauseHotkey: Pauses or resumes OCR scanning everywhere. Useful during cutscenes or when you want OCR temporarily out of the way. Press Escape to clear.
tooltips.processPriority: Sets the process priority for the OCR Python process on Windows. Higher values may improve OCR responsiveness but can reduce system responsiveness.
tooltips.defaultSceneFurigana: Used when a scene does not yet have a saved config file. Existing scenes keep their own saved value.
tooltips.obsCapturePreprocess: Applies preprocessing to OBS screenshots before OCR cropping. Grayscale + Autocontrast + Unsharp can improve subtitle OCR in noisy scenes.
tooltips.ignoreRun1Logs: Hides OCR terminal lines containing "OCR Run 1: Text recognized..." to reduce duplicate output noise when two-pass OCR is enabled. Run 2 lines stay highlighted in green.
tooltips.installDependency: Install optional OCR dependencies into the GSM Python environment. If unsure, choose a recommended option.
tooltips.uninstallDependency: Remove optional OCR dependencies from the GSM Python environment.
tooltips.replacements: Open the OCR replacement rules page for fixing recurring OCR mistakes after recognition.
tooltips.openConfigFile: Open the active Electron OCR config JSON file.
tooltips.openConfigFolder: Open the OCR config folder that stores per-scene area files.
tooltips.openGlobalConfig: Open the global OWOCR config used by the OCR engines.
tooltips.openTempFolder: Open GSM's temp folder to see OCR'd image samples and helper files.
tooltips.clearConsole: Clear the OCR console output shown in this tab.
tooltips.startAuto: Start continuous OCR scanning for the selected scene's rectangles. GSM will keep polling and send lines automatically.
tooltips.startManual: Start manual OCR mode. GSM waits for your manual capture hotkeys instead of scanning continuously.
tooltips.stop: Stop the active OCR process completely.
tooltips.pause: Pause automatic OCR scanning without closing the OCR process.
tooltips.resume: Resume OCR scanning after it has been paused.
tooltips.advancedRecognition: Engine, scan, and capture settings that control how OCR runs.
tooltips.comparison: Dedupe and change-detection thresholds that decide when text is new, stable, or a subset of a previous line.

notices.areaConfigExported: OCR area config copied to clipboard.

textProcessing.title: Text Preprocessing
textProcessing.subtitle: Configure text processing rules applied to captured game text before it reaches Anki. Drag to reorder processing pipeline.
textProcessing.dragToReorder: Drag items to change execution order. Processors run top-to-bottom.
textProcessing.previewPlaceholder: Paste game text here to test your processing rules...

processors.stringReplacementDesc: Custom find-and-replace rules (plain text or regex)
processors.removeNonJapaneseDesc: Filter out characters not in the Shift-JIS character set
processors.removeNewlinesDesc: Join all lines into a single line separated by spaces
processors.extractBracketedTextDesc: Keep only text between Japanese quotation marks 「...」
processors.extractLinesDesc: Keep only a specified number of lines from the text
